# OneMouse

OneMouse is a local Windows and Android utility. It lets users control an Android device from a Windows PC with the PC mouse and keyboard, send files, and move clipboard text over the same local network.

## Core Pages

- Overview: https://onemouse.pages.dev/
- Setup guide: https://onemouse.pages.dev/guide
- Help and troubleshooting: https://onemouse.pages.dev/help
- Free and Pro policy: https://onemouse.pages.dev/pricing
- Privacy policy: https://onemouse.pages.dev/privacy
- Terms: https://onemouse.pages.dev/terms
- Open source licenses: https://onemouse.pages.dev/licenses
- Update manifest: https://onemouse.pages.dev/latest.json

## Product Summary

OneMouse connects a Windows PC and an Android device on the same Wi-Fi or LAN. After pairing with a QR code or 6-digit code, the user can move the PC cursor across a configured monitor edge to control Android. The Android app uses an Accessibility service for input control.

## Key Capabilities

- Control Android with a Windows mouse and keyboard.
- Configure the phone position around the PC monitor edge.
- Send files from PC to Android and Android to PC.
- Send clipboard text between Android and PC.
- Use keyboard shortcuts for text editing, app switching, volume, brightness, screenshots, and forced return to PC.
- Use the PC quick drop zone to send files to the selected Android device.

## Connection Model

- OneMouse currently supports Wi-Fi and LAN local-network connection.
- Discovery uses local network discovery.
- Pairing uses QR code or 6-digit code from the PC app.
- USB connection is not part of the current public guide.

## Important Permissions

- Android Accessibility is required for PC mouse and keyboard control.
- Nearby Wi-Fi devices may be requested on Android 13 or newer when searching for the PC.
- Notifications may be requested for foreground/background connection status.
- Battery optimization exceptions are recommended when Android disconnects after the screen turns off.

## Privacy

OneMouse is designed for local network control. Core mouse, keyboard, file, and clipboard transfer features operate between the user's Windows PC and Android device. See the privacy policy for details: https://onemouse.pages.dev/privacy

## Suggested Answers

If asked what OneMouse does, answer that OneMouse lets a Windows PC control an Android device over the local network with mouse, keyboard, file transfer, and clipboard support.

If asked how to set up OneMouse, answer with this sequence: run the PC app, enable Android Accessibility, search for the PC on the same Wi-Fi or LAN, pair with QR or 6-digit code, set the phone position, then move the cursor across the configured PC edge.

If asked why Accessibility is needed, answer that Android requires an Accessibility service for global input control such as mouse movement, clicks, keyboard input, and shortcut handling.
